title: feat(ibg-repository): observational repository proposal evaluation (IBG-1 PR F)
intent: Introduce a deterministic, OBSERVATIONAL evaluation layer that analyzes a RepositoryChangeProposal together with its RepositoryExecutionPlan and reports whether the pair is structurally complete, internally consistent, and continuous with the evidence provenance it claims — before a human reviewer spends attention on it. The evaluation scores, classifies, and reports; it never edits a proposal or plan, generates a commit, executes git, creates a pull request, alters governance, promotes evidence, or authorizes engineering. A failed check is a reported observation, NOT a rejection or a withheld approval: the human review boundary established by PR A-E is unchanged. The evaluation is content-addressed (eval- id), byte-stable for identical artifacts, and preserves provenance exactly. It is a SEPARATE downstream consumer of the merged proposal + plan contracts: it does not modify, wrap, or attach to the PR-D RepositoryProposalPipeline or the PR-E ExecutionPlanner, and nothing evaluates implicitly as a side effect.
behavior_change: Adds two additive modules (repository_proposal_evaluation.py contract + proposal_evaluator.py evaluator) plus their tests under the existing services/api/app/ibg_repository/ package, and additively re-exports the PR-F API from __init__.py. No existing runtime path imports the evaluator; no existing behavior, schema, authority, or governance changes. One behavior-PRESERVING refactor in cbsp21_patch_adapter.py: the gate's behavior-change articulation rule is extracted into validate_behavior_change_articulation() (with the threshold named as WHY_NOT_REDUNDANT_MIN_CHARS) and is now CALLED by validate_cbsp21_patch_packet, which remains the whole-packet authority. The extraction exists so the evaluator can report that single rule as a discrete finding by REUSING the validator rather than re-implementing its threshold — one rule, one implementation. validate_cbsp21_patch_packet's accepted/rejected inputs and error messages are unchanged apart from the threshold now being interpolated from the named constant, and the existing PR-A adapter tests cover it unchanged. The evaluator derives every result mechanically from the two artifacts: 25 structural checks across five categories (completeness, governance, provenance, execution, invariant), each owned by exactly ONE category and never duplicated across lists; completeness_score is an auditable checks_passed/checks_applicable ratio computed in decimal and serialized as a fixed-precision string, with not_applicable checks excluded from the denominator and an empty denominator failing closed; readiness_summary is 'complete'/'incomplete' derived ONLY from the failed-applicable count. Fail-closed (ProposalEvaluationError) ONLY on artifacts that cannot be coherently evaluated: wrong type, None, a plan describing a different proposal, no applicable checks, or content that cannot be canonically serialized. Every substantive readiness gap in an evaluable artifact is REPORTED as a failed finding rather than raised. An AST-precise test forbids the modules from importing subprocess/os/shutil/tempfile/network/GitHub clients or the PR-B git_runner and worktree machinery, and a further test asserts the evaluator imports and calls no plan/pipeline builder and is not wired into the pipeline.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Creates repository_proposal_evaluation.py: the frozen, content-addressed RepositoryProposalEvaluation dataclass plus the frozen EvaluationFinding record, the readiness/category/status vocabularies (pass|fail|not_applicable — deliberately NO warn state, because severity is the reviewer's judgment), ProposalEvaluationError, compute_completeness_score() (Decimal, quantized two places ROUND_HALF_UP, serialized as a fixed-precision string so the content hash can never depend on binary float representation; fails closed on an empty denominator), sort_findings() (deterministic (category, check_id) ordering so incidental evaluation order never leaks into identity), summarize_findings(), and to_canonical_dict()/to_audit_dict()/compute_evaluation_hash() with an evaluation_id derived as 'eval-' + sha256(canonical_content)[:16] (created_at excluded from identity and canonical form). Creates proposal_evaluator.py: evaluate_repository_proposal(proposal, plan), evaluate_execution_plan() (the plan-facing findings only, sharing the same logic), the stateless ProposalEvaluator (single 'evaluate' operation), proposal_evaluator() factory, and validate_repository_proposal_evaluation(). The 25 checks delegate to the existing validators rather than forking their rule sets: validate_cbsp21_patch_packet for packet validity, the extracted validate_behavior_change_articulation for the articulation rule, PR-E's SUPPORTED_RISK_LEVELS for declared risk, the proposal builder's _file_within_authorized containment semantics for the authorized boundary, and the governance AuthorityState enum for recognized authority labels. cbsp21_patch_adapter.py gains the extracted validate_behavior_change_articulation() + WHY_NOT_REDUNDANT_MIN_CHARS, both additively re-exported. __init__ additively re-exports the PR-F API. conftest gains make_plan/evaluation_of fixtures building REAL governed artifacts. Two test modules (107 tests) bind: stable/byte-identical content-addressed id, JSON round-trip, audit-timestamp exclusion, fixed-precision decimal score across a parametrized table, not_applicable excluded from the denominator, empty-denominator fail-closed, deterministic finding ordering, one-owning-category partition, counts agreeing with findings, every hard-error input raising, every evaluable defect REPORTING a failed finding instead (provenance discontinuity/absence/inexactness, unsupported risk, invalid CBSP21, unarticulated behavior change, unreadable packet, files outside the authorized boundary, reclassified proposal/plan, plan id disagreeing with its content, stray group file, omitted declared file, invented undeclared file, diverging/empty validation sequence, missing complexity), readiness derived only from failed applicable checks, exact provenance continuity, every recognized authority state passing while an invented one fails, proposal and plan unmutated, authority state unchanged, no decision vocabulary or verb on the contract or the evaluator, the evaluator not wired into the pipeline, and the AST-precise no-git/filesystem/GitHub/network import invariant. New-module coverage is 99% (repository_proposal_evaluation.py 100%, proposal_evaluator.py 99% — the two uncovered lines are a defensive canonical-serialization guard unreachable while all canonical content is str/int). The architecture doc is added under docs/architecture, including how a reviewer should read an evaluation and what the score explicitly does not mean.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No evaluation layer existed. PR A-E shipped the proposal, worktree, review-bundle, thin orchestration, and descriptive execution-planning contracts; each terminates at the human-review boundary, and none of them answers whether a proposal + plan pair is internally complete, governed, and provenance-continuous before a reviewer opens it. This layer composes the existing merged contracts and derives only what those artifacts already declare: it invents no score, no severity, no confidence, and no judgment, duplicates no proposal/planning/review logic, and does not modify or attach to the PR-D pipeline or the PR-E planner. It is not redundant with the fail-closed builders: those enforce their preconditions at construction time, whereas the contracts are plain frozen dataclasses that can be constructed directly, so the evaluator deliberately checks the artifacts AS GIVEN rather than assuming a builder produced them — an independent check, not a restatement of the builders' preconditions. It preserves provenance and the human-review boundary and adds a distinct downstream capability rather than re-implementing an existing one.

commands_run: python -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/test_cbsp21_patch_adapter.py --no-cov -q  ->  15 passed (adds the gate-threshold AST drift guard binding WHY_NOT_REDUNDANT_MIN_CHARS to the gate, and the 19/20/21 articulation boundary case; the extracted rule and its whole-packet caller agree at the boundary)

File-size decomposition: proposal_evaluator.py (was 794 lines, over the 500 god-object target) split into proposal_evaluator.py (314 lines: orchestration + public API) + proposal_checks.py (496 lines: the 25 check builders). Pure code move, behavior-identical — all 122 PR-F tests pass unchanged, including every content-hash/determinism assertion, so evaluation ids are byte-identical to the pre-split evaluator. No package file now exceeds 500 lines; the repo large-file count returns to its target of 99. The one updated test (test_evaluator_does_not_attach_to_the_pipeline_or_the_planner) now asserts the no-pipeline/no-builder invariant over BOTH layer files, strengthening it.

python -m coverage run -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/test_proposal_evaluator.py tests/ibg_repository/test_repository_proposal_evaluation.py --no-cov -q  &&  coverage report --include=*/ibg_repository/proposal_evaluator.py,*/ibg_repository/repository_proposal_evaluation.py  ->  99% (contract 100%, evaluator 99%)

python -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/ --no-cov -q  ->  465 passed (PR A + B + C + D + E + F together, no regressions). Rebase evidence: this branch was rebased onto the squash-merged PR-E (origin/main @ 3d23c667) with 'git rebase --onto origin/main 094c4b85', replaying only F's six commits; a plain rebase would have replayed E's five pre-squash commits, whose patch-ids a squash merge does not preserve. Measured baseline: origin/main collects 358 tests in tests/ibg_repository/; this branch collects 465; net +107, exactly F's own two test modules and no others. F's six commits and every F-owned file (proposal_evaluator.py, repository_proposal_evaluation.py, their two test modules, and the __init__/conftest/cbsp21_patch_adapter edits, plus the architecture doc) are byte-identical through the rebase, verified with 'git diff pre-rebase-f-backup HEAD -- <those paths>' returning empty. This manifest is the sole deliberate exception: its verification evidence was refreshed AFTER the rebase to replace the now-superseded pre-rebase counts, so it alone differs from pre-rebase-f-backup, and that difference is this evidence block. E-owned files (execution_planner.py, its tests, IBG_EXECUTION_PLANNING.md, the execution-planning manifest) changed ONLY because F previously sat on a pre-review E and now sits on the reviewed, squash-merged E implementation; F's work did not change and its 107 tests pass unmodified against that upgraded substrate. The superseded pre-rebase count was 438 against the older E.

findings.title: Evaluation could drift into decision-making
findings.detail: An evaluator that reports readiness is one vocabulary change away from approving. Mitigated structurally rather than by convention: the classification is observational_proposal_evaluation__no_approval_authority; the readiness vocabulary is limited to complete/incomplete and the finding vocabulary to pass/fail/not_applicable (no warn state, since severity is a reviewer judgment); the score is a bare structural ratio with no weighting; there is no free-form reviewer_notes field; the evaluator exposes exactly one operation ('evaluate') and no approve/reject/block/authorize/merge/commit/push/execute verb; and tests assert the absence of that vocabulary on both the contract and the evaluator.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Additive, observational-only. Derives every result from the already-merged proposal + plan; invents no score, severity, confidence, or judgment. Separate downstream consumer — does not modify or attach to the PR-D pipeline or the PR-E planner, and is not wired into either. No git/filesystem/GitHub/network/authority. Terminates at the human-review boundary: a failed check informs a reviewer and blocks nothing. The single cbsp21_patch_adapter.py change is a behavior-preserving extraction made specifically to REUSE the existing validator instead of duplicating its rule, which the dev order requires. Any execution engine remains out of scope.
